# UC-18.1.5 · Cisco ACI Tenant Configuration Audit — Intent vs Realized State, Snapshot Lifecycle, and Per-Tenant Blast Radius

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Audit, Change, Compliance, Configuration &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We keep a plain record of who changed each tenant slice on your network fabric, when it happened, and whether the work fully finished everywhere—so surprise rewires cannot hide behind everyday automation noise.*

---

## Description

Splunk audits Cisco ACI tenant-scope configuration by anchoring **fvTenant** containers under **uni/tn-{tenant}** and tracing **polUni** policy-universe propagation so intent separates cleanly from dataplane realization. APIC **aaaModLR** Audit Log MO rows expose **descr**, **affected**, **user**, **changeSet**, **txId**—filter **affected** DNs containing **tn-{tenant}** so CRUD stays per-tenant across **fvBD**, **fvAEPg**, **vzBrCP**, **vzFilter**, and **fvRsCons**/**fvRsProv** bindings. **configJob** exports fuel offline/Git snapshot diff while **configRollback** narrates restore audits. Immediacy (**on-demand** vs **immediate**) explains realized lag; syslog pairs **AAA-5-CONFIG_CHANGE** with fault **F0467** deployed-config-mismatch under **pmodVns** modes. REST PATCH traces on `/api/mo/uni/tn-{tenant}.xml` plus APIC **configChange** sequencing complement audits without drifting into fabric-wide scorecards (**UC-18.1.1**) or multi-vendor SDN journals (**UC-18.3.8**).

## Value

Tenant-scoped edits silently reorder subnets and contracts—Splunk stitches **aaaModLR** narratives to **configJob** lifecycle, tags immediacy-driven lag, scores blast radius per **txId**, and couples login evidence so unauthorized moves surface before dataplane mismatches widen.

## Implementation

**Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** ([Splunkbase **4022**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022)) lands syslog-normalized **`cisco:aci:audit`**, **`cisco:aci:syslog`**, **`cisco:aci:config`** lines; schedule HTTPS collectors authenticated via **`aaaLogin.json`**/**`aaaRefresh.json`** polling **`aaaModLR`**, **`configJob`**, **`configRollback`**, **`fvTenant`** subtrees into the same operational index—codify **`FIELDALIAS`** macros, **`tenant_chg_windows`** suppression lookups, and dashboard tokens **`tok_tenant`**/**`tok_tx`**.

**Quick win:** hourly **`index=cisco_aci`** alert on **`cisco:aci:audit`** rows where **`risky_after_hours=1`** without an approved **`CHG`** token—surfaces rogue edits fast.

## Evidence

Saved search **`aci_uc1815_tenant_audit_risk`** on **`index=cisco_aci`** (**`cisco:aci:audit`**, **`cisco:aci:syslog`**, **`cisco:aci:config`**) plus Dashboard Studio workbook **`ds_aci_tenant_audit.json`** wired with **`tok_tenant`**/**`tok_tx`** drill tokens.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Cisco APIC clusters on supported 6.0(x)/6.1(x) trains must expose HTTPS 443/tcp to Splunk-connected collectors; dedicate read-only automation identities isolated from GUI admins because overlapping aaaRefresh.json rotations collide when scripted collectors compete with interactive sessions.
- Install Splunk_TA_cisco-aci ([Splunkbase 4022](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022)) wherever APIC and fabric syslog terminates—preserve distinct sourcetypes **cisco:aci:audit**, **cisco:aci:syslog**, **cisco:aci:config** so parsers remain auditable.
- Layer Splunk REST API Modular Input (rest_ta, Splunkbase 1546) or hardened Python modular collectors executing POST /api/aaaLogin.json and POST /api/aaaRefresh.json before class pulls—stamp collector_id metadata whenever multiple inputs authenticate toward the same APIC VIP.
- Capacity planning remains sensitive because dense **aaaModLR** pulls elevate APIC CPU—shard collectors per fabric POD when syslog EPS spikes linger near negotiated envelopes.

**Step 1 — Data sources**
(1) Poll **aaaModLR** via GET https://<apic>/api/node/class/aaaModLR.json?query-target-filter=and(wcard(aaaModLR.dn,"tn-"))&order-by=aaaModLR.created|desc so tenant-scoped audit rows remain newest-first; widen filters cautiously—APIC CPU climbs when wildcards wrap entire uni trees.
(2) Harvest **configJob** via GET /api/node/class/configJob.json to capture configuration-export snapshots that feed offline differencing—pair completion timestamps with Git commit hashes emitted by nightly exporters.
(3) Track **configRollback** via GET /api/node/class/configRollback.json for snapshot-restore audits—correlate rollback_snapshot_id values with concurrent faultInst accrual without collapsing this forensic lens into APIC quorum drills (UC-18.1.7).
(4) Diff tenant intent using GET /api/node/class/fvTenant.json?query-target=children&target-subtree-class=fvAp,fvBD,fvCtx,vzBrCP,fvBcpc on a 60–300s cadence so fvBD, fvAEPg, vzBrCP, vzFilter, and vzAny-adjacent objects remain comparable across polls—do not confuse these traversals with physical-interface saturation (UC-18.1.6) or contract counter hunts (UC-18.1.4).
(5) Steer APIC facility=local6 syslog through Splunk_TA_cisco-aci while preserving AAA-5-CONFIG_CHANGE narratives alongside fault F0467 deployed-config-mismatch stories referencing tn-{tenant} fragments within Distinguished Names.
(6) Optionally call Nexus Dashboard Insights GET https://<nd-insights>/sedgeapi/v1/site_status/auditLogs (OAuth-protected) to merge per-site audit widgets with Splunk—keep site_code tokens synchronized between dashboards.
(7) Tie configuration exports to Git using APIC scheduled tasks or CI pushes so **configJob** payloads land in repositories; ingest commit webhooks or polled JSON so changeSet excerpts align with merge subjects.

**Step 2 — Field extractions**
After **fieldsummary** baselines land, codify **FIELDALIAS** macros covering tenant-facing columns such as tenant_name, affected_dn, change_action synonyms, user_name, tx_id, change_set snapshots, change_ts, source_ip, session_id, mode cues mined from **aaaModLR.descr**, config_job_id, rollback_snapshot_id, realized_state buckets, mo_class_changed literals spanning **fvBD**, **fvAEPg**, **vzBrCP**, **vzFilter**, **fvRsCons**, **fvRsProv**, affected_count, and policy_immediacy selectors contrasting **on-demand** rollout against **immediate** pushes.

**Step 3 — Initial validation**
Cross-check Splunk _time ordering against APIC Admin → Audit Log filtered to tn-{tenant}; gaps wider than twice the poll interval hint duplicate collectors or stale aaaRefresh cookies. Compare Insights audit widgets against Splunk partitions keyed by tenant_name. When operators run acidiag dbcc audit-export diagnostics, compare SHA256 hashes against configJob JSON indexed as cisco:aci:config. When Git exporters run, verify commit subjects echo the txId captured inside aaaModLR rows. Stretched-site deployments should compare Splunk timelines per site_code—Nexus Dashboard Orchestrator backlog can briefly trail primary-site aaaModLR surges without implying malicious edits, yet still demands documentation in the same incident record.

**Step 4 — SPL building blocks**
Union **cisco:aci:audit**, **cisco:aci:syslog**, and **cisco:aci:config** early, normalize Distinguished Names via **rex**/**eval** helpers, derive **change-velocity** per **tenant_name** per **user_name** hourly bins, compute **perc95(change_events)** baselines for burst detection, flag **risky_after_hours** outside CMDB **tenant_chg_windows**, compute **blast_radius** as **dc(affected_dn)**+**dc(mo_class_changed)** constrained per **tx_id**, score **intent_vs_realized_lag** by contrasting **policy_immediacy** with **realized_state**, and pair **rollback_snapshot_id** restores with preceding **tx_id** edits.

**Step 5 — Comprehensive enrichment patterns (deepest section)**
- AAA correlation — join **user_name** rows from **cisco:aci:audit** with **tacacs:session** or **cisco:acs:radius** successes inside two-minute envelopes so analysts distinguish GUI shells from automation principals; absent pairs escalate identity threads even when **faultInst** stays quiet.
- Tenant ownership overlays — nightly **tenant_owner** lookups map **tenant_name** to custodians, escalation aliases, and **pmodVns** tenancy semantics so paging routes cite humans instead of anonymous **APIC** identifiers.
- CHG pairing — MID-tier **CHG** extracts or replicated tables expose **correlation_display** tokens; Splunk macros scan **descr** fragments for embedded **tx_id** mentions so **after_hours** bursts inherit CAB metadata automatically.
- Git/Ansible lineage — Cisco **DCNM** and **ACI** Ansible modules stamp **ansible_job_id**/**git_commit** strings—lookup tables bind repository hashes to automation bursts so CI pushes never resemble rogue edits.
- Vault posture — optional **vault_audit** feeds reveal **svc_aci** lease rotations—pair renewals with **Authentication** successes before treating dormant sessions as **T1098** account manipulation paired with tenant edits.
- MITRE overlays — annotate **T1078** valid-account reuse whenever dormant identities suddenly author **aaaModLR** rows, and note **T1098** suspicion whenever **user_name** rotates without HR attestations.
- CIM Change adoption — accelerate **Change** summaries once **FIELDALIAS** maps **object**←**affected_dn**, **command**←**change_action**, **user**←**user_name**, **src**←**source_ip**—keep raw indexes because MO payloads exceed **tstats** brevity.
- Splunk Enterprise Security — optional risk modifiers elevate scores when **blast_radius** crosses tenant baselines while **Authentication** correlation gaps persist—route adaptive responses through **tenant_owner** lists.
- Rollback pairing — compare **configRollback** failures against subsequent **F0467** waves scoped to **tenant_name** so half-finished restores surface before faults mushroom.
- Intent-vs-realized pairing — reconcile **fvRsCons**/**fvRsProv** attachments against **vzBrCP** scopes whenever **aaaModLR** hints partial publishes—spot dangling contracts without drifting into permit counters (**UC-18.1.4**).
- polUni propagation cues — chart delays between **aaaModLR.created** timestamps and **fvTenant** subtree polls when diagnosing backlog—not fabric-wide scores (**UC-18.1.1**).
- REST PATCH narratives — retain `/api/mo/uni/tn-{tenant}.xml` verbs beside **changeSet** excerpts so auditors replay intent versus dataplane faults (**F0467**) inside one Splunk workspace.
- Documentation hygiene — whenever suppress macros quiet CI bursts, attach Markdown notes beside macros so reviewers inherit rationale without resurrecting stale chat histories.

**Step 6 — Operational runbook**
Unauthorized-change Sev-2 paging fires when privileged identities mutate **vzBrCP**/**fvBD** rows without CHG coverage—embed blast_radius, tx_id, mode, and rollback_snapshot_id inside PagerDuty payloads referencing the originating **aaaModLR** stanza. After-hours Sev-3 review queues risky_after_hours positives for tenant owners whenever CHG metadata conflicts with Splunk—prevents noisy escalations. Operational MTBF stays at or above 1296 hours between nuisance pages once suppress macros stabilize; MTTR remains at or below 45 minutes from acknowledgement to reconciled aaaModLR proof or Cisco TAC escalation when APIC refuses closure. Dashboard tokens tok_tenant, tok_user, tok_tx feed per-tenant change-velocity charts, top-N changers, blast-radius heatmaps, after-hours timelines, and intent-vs-realized lag plots contrasting policy_immediacy with realized_state hues. Weekly hygiene rotates tenant_chg_windows CSVs, refreshes FIELDALIAS suites whenever Splunk_TA_cisco-aci upgrades shift camelCase, regression-tests HTTPS collectors whenever APIC returns HTTP 429 storms, and archives screenshots whenever Splunk surfaces divergence ahead of GUI refresh cycles.

**Quick win:** Hourly alert on **index=cisco_aci** sourcetype=cisco:aci:audit where **risky_after_hours=1** with no matching **CHG** lookup surfaces rogue edits before fault accrual.

**Troubleshooting modes:** duplicated collectors authenticated to one VIP double-count **aaaModLR** rows until **collector_id** deduplication joins arrive; camelCase drift after TA upgrades blanks **tx_id** until **FIELDALIAS** refresh; syslog-only narratives without REST corroboration may omit MO identifiers—never declare outages solely from syslog strings; Insights OAuth expiry yields sparse **auditLogs** panels although Splunk REST feeds remain rich—cross-verify OAuth secrets quarterly; Git exporters occasionally squash commits—confirm SHA parity against **configJob** payloads before blaming ingest gaps. APIC scheduler throttling sometimes pauses subtree polls while syslog still streams AAA events—when _indextime minus _time skew exceeds agreed SLAs, inspect Heavy Forwarder queues before rewriting macros. Duplicate **tx_id** values can appear when MO export jobs restart—dedupe using **ind** plus **created** timestamps from the REST payload.


## SPL

```spl
index=cisco_aci (sourcetype="cisco:aci:audit" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:syslog" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:config") earliest=-24h@m
| eval lane=case(sourcetype=="cisco:aci:audit","audit_line",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:syslog","syslog_line",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:config","config_job_line",1=1,"other")
| eval syslog_audit_hit=if(sourcetype=="cisco:aci:syslog" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(AAA-5-CONFIG_CHANGE|aaaModLR|F0467|deployed-config-mismatch|CONFIG_CHANGE|MODLR)"),1,0)
| where lane!="syslog_line" OR syslog_audit_hit=1
| eval tenant_name=lower(trim(coalesce(tenant_name,tenant,tn)))
| rex field=affected_dn "(?i)uni/tn-(?<tenant_dn>[^/]+)"
| rex field=affected "(?i)uni/tn-(?<tenant_af>[^/]+)"
| rex max_match=0 "(?i)uni/tn-(?<tenant_raw>[^/]+)"
| eval tenant_name=coalesce(if(len(tenant_name)>0 AND tenant_name!="unknown",tenant_name,null()),tenant_dn,tenant_af,tenant_raw,"unknown_tenant")
| eval affected_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(affected_dn,affected,distinguished_name,"")))
| eval change_action=lower(trim(coalesce(change_action,action,audit_action,operation,"")))
| eval user_name=trim(coalesce(user_name,user,audit_user,aaa_user,session_user,""))
| eval tx_id=trim(coalesce(tx_id,txId,transaction_id,change_txn,ind))
| eval mo_class_changed=lower(trim(coalesce(mo_class_changed,classId,mo_class,object_class,"")))
| eval realized_state=lower(trim(coalesce(realized_state,deploy_status,job_status,rollback_status,"")))
| eval policy_immediacy=lower(trim(coalesce(policy_immediacy,deploy_immediacy,immediacy,"immediate")))
| eval hour_bucket=strftime(_time,"%H")
| eval dow=lower(strftime(_time,"%A"))
| eval after_hours=if((hour_bucket>=22 OR hour_bucket<=5 OR dow IN ("saturday","sunday")),1,0)
| lookup tenant_chg_windows tenant_name OUTPUT window_start window_end maint_tag
| eval inside_chg_window=if(isnotnull(window_start) AND _time>=window_start AND _time<=window_end,1,0)
| eval risky_after_hours=if(after_hours=1 AND inside_chg_window=0,1,0)
| bin _time span=1h as hr
| stats dc(affected_lc) as affected_dn_count dc(mo_class_changed) as mo_family_count count as change_events sum(risky_after_hours) as after_hours_events latest(realized_state) as realized_latest latest(policy_immediacy) as immediacy_latest values(change_action) as action_vals latest(tx_id) as tx_seen by tenant_name user_name hr tx_id
| eval blast_radius=affected_dn_count+mo_family_count
| eval action_diversity=mvcount(action_vals)
| eval intent_vs_realized_lag=case(
    immediacy_latest IN ("immediate","fast") AND match(realized_latest,"pending|wait|defer|schedule"),2,
    immediacy_latest IN ("on-demand","lazy","delayed") AND match(realized_latest,"deployed|applied|program"),1,
    realized_latest IN ("error","failed","fault"),3,
    1=1,0)
| eventstats perc95(change_events) as p95_chg by tenant_name
| eval velocity_flag=if(change_events>=coalesce(p95_chg,change_events)*1.75 OR change_events>=18,1,0)
| where blast_radius>=4 OR velocity_flag=1 OR after_hours_events>=1 OR intent_vs_realized_lag>=2 OR action_diversity>6
| sort - blast_radius - change_events - intent_vs_realized_lag
| head 280
| table hr tenant_name user_name tx_id change_events blast_radius affected_dn_count mo_family_count after_hours_events intent_vs_realized_lag immediacy_latest realized_latest action_vals
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(_time) as lt values(Change.object) as obj values(Change.action) as ca count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes where nodename=Change.All_Changes by Change.user Change.src Change.object
| join Change.user max=0 [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(_time) as auth_t values(Authentication.user) as au values(Authentication.src) as asrc count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication where nodename=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user Authentication.src]
| eval actor_gap=if(isnull(auth_t) OR abs(lt-auth_t)>120,"missing_auth_pair","paired")
| where actor_gap="missing_auth_pair"
| sort - lt
| head 250
```

## Visualization

(1) Timechart — **per-tenant change-velocity** (**change_events**/hour). (2) Column chart — **top-N changers** (**user_name**). (3) Heatmap — **tenant_name** × **mo_class_changed** **blast_radius**. (4) Timeline — **after_hours_events** spikes vs **`maint_tag`**. (5) Scatter — **intent_vs_realized_lag** versus **policy_immediacy** with **realized_state** hues.

## Known False Positives

**Planned tenant decommissioning during application retire windows** — CAB-sanctioned waves delete **fvAEPg**/**fvBD** hierarchies rapidly, so Splunk velocity and **blast_radius** resemble abuse even while traffic drains by design; require **CHG** correlation and **tenant_retire** suppress flags.

**CD-pipeline orchestrated config refreshes from approved Ansible repos** — Jenkins or **ACI** Ansible modules emit dense **REST** bursts with shared service principals; whitelist **git_commit**/**ansible_job_id** lookups and **svc_aci_cd** identities so velocity panels stay quiet.

**Deliberate snapshot-restore drills** — engineers trigger **configRollback** plus **configJob** replays during **DR** exercises, inflating rollback pairings without production impact; stamp **drill_id** tokens in CMDB overlays before paging **Sev-2**.

**Bulk EPG-import bursts during onboarding sprints** — application teams import dozens of **fvAEPg** rows in one maintenance slice, stretching **aaaModLR** transcripts; align with documented **cutover_id** milestones and mute only when **blast_radius** stays inside predeclared ceilings.

**Tenant-clone replication toward staging fabrics** — **MSO**/**NDO** operations copy tenants for environment parity; **txId** sequencing mirrors production yet targets non-prod indexes—partition Splunk indexes per **site_code** so staging noise never masquerades as production tampering.
